- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object

Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
List all available schemas. Tell me what typenames are available.

content.text: The user wants to see all available schemas. Let me list them.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent invocations of the trigger. For Feed triggers, this will process Feed items in parallel.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

response.delta: me organize the schemas by their typenames and kinds.

Here are all the available schemas and their typenames:

### Object Types
ando` | A flexible/generic object that allows

additional properties |
| `org.dxos.type.blueprint` | AI assistant blueprint with instructions

, tools, and MCP servers |
| `org.dxos.type.feed` | A feed (

data stream) with name, kind, and namespace |
| `org.dxos.type.function` | A function definition

with input/output schemas, versioning, and bindings |
| `org.dxos.type.organization` | An organization with name, description, status

, image, website, and location |
| `org.dxos.type.person` | A person with full name, emails, phone

numbers, addresses, job info, etc. |
| `org.dxos.type.prompt` | A prompt template with input

/output schemas, instructions, and blueprints |
| `org.dxos.type.tag` | A tag with a label and optional hue (color

) |
| `org.dxos.type.trigger` | A trigger for functions (email, queue, subscription, timer

, or webhook) |
| `org.dxos.type.view` | A view with a query and field projection |

### Relation Types
| Typename | Description |
|

) → **Organization** (target), with role, active status, and dates |

---
enames** and **1 relation typename** available. The most commonly used data types are **Person
**, and **View** support automation and AI workflows.

'll create the "Project" schema with the specified fields right away.

tools.description:
Adds the object to the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are useful long-term for the conversation.

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

Removes the object from the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are no longer useful for the conversation.

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: delete-object
tools.description: Deletes the object.

obj.description:

- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: update-object

obj.description:

- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Create a preson fullName="John Doe" with a reference to the organization "Cyberdyne Systems".

content.text:
The user wants to create a person with fullName="John Doe" and a reference to the organization "Cyberdyne Systems". I need to:

1. Find the schema for person and organization types
2. Find the organization "Cyberdyne Systems"
3. Create the person with a reference to that organization

Let me start by listing schemas and searching for the organization.
I'll start by looking up the available schemas and searching for the "Cyberdyne Systems" organization in parallel.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent invocations of the trigger. For Feed triggers, this will process Feed items in parallel.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

response.delta: search for "Cyberdyne Systems" returned no results. I
need to create the organization first, and then create the person

with a reference to it.

Looking at the person schema (`org.dxos.type.person`), the
`organization` field is a reference to an `org.dxos.type.organization` object.
Let me first create the organization, then create the person with a reference to it.
was found, so I'll create it first, then use its reference for the person.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Create a preson fullName="John Doe" with a reference to the organization "Cyberdyne Systems".

content.text:
The user wants to create a person with fullName="John Doe" and a reference to the organization "Cyberdyne Systems". I need to:

1. Find the schema for person and organization types
2. Find the organization "Cyberdyne Systems"
3. Create the person with a reference to that organization

Let me start by listing schemas and searching for the organization.
I'll start by looking up the available schemas and searching for the "Cyberdyne Systems" organization in parallel.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent invocations of the trigger. For Feed triggers, this will process Feed items in parallel.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

content.text:
The search for "Cyberdyne Systems" returned no results. I need to create the organization first, and then create the person with a reference to it.

Looking at the person schema (`org.dxos.type.person`), the `organization` field is a reference to an `org.dxos.type.organization` object.

Let me first create the organization, then create the person with a reference to it.
No existing "Cyberdyne Systems" organization was found, so I'll create it first, then use its reference for the person.
Now I have the organization created with DXN `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTHJ`. Now I'll create the person with a reference to this organization.
The organization has been created. Now I'll create the person with a reference to it.

response.delta:
! Here's a summary of what was created:

1. **Organization** — **Cyberdyne Systems** (`dxn:echo:B
with the `organization` field referencing Cyberdyne Systems.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

</blueprint>

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent invocations of the trigger. For Feed triggers, this will process Feed items in parallel.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.

content.text: The user wants to create a relation between two objects. Let me first list schemas to find the relation typename, and also search for the two objects mentioned.
I'll start by listing the schemas to find the right relation typename, and simultaneously search for both "John Connor" and "Cyberdyne Systems" in the space.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Create an Employer relation from person "John Connor" to organization "Cyberdyne Systems" with role "Engineer". List schemas first to find the relation typename.

content.text: The user wants to create a relation between two objects. Let me first list schemas to find the relation typename, and also search for the two objects mentioned.
I'll start by listing the schemas to find the right relation typename, and simultaneously search for both "John Connor" and "Cyberdyne Systems" in the space.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An employing organization of a person.

key.description: Unique registration key for the blueprint

jsonSchema.description: An organization.
jsonSchema.title: Organization

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

concurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent invocations of the trigger. For Feed triggers, this will process Feed items in parallel.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

content.text:
I found:

1. **Schema**: `org.dxos.relation.employer` — a relation type with source: `org.dxos.type.person` and target: `org.dxos.type.organization`. Properties include `role`, `active`, `startDate`, `endDate`.

2. **John Connor** (Person): `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTY3`


3. **Cyberdyne Systems** (Organization): `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTY5`

Now I have both entities I need to establish the employment relation, with John Connor as the source and Cyberdyne Systems as the target.
I found the relation schema `org.dxos.relation.employer` (source: Person → target: Organization), and both objects. Now I'll create the relation.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>
Delete the relation dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV2X.

content.text: The user wants to delete a relation with the DXN `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV2X`.

tools.name: add-to-context
tools.description:
Adds the object to the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are useful long-term for the conversation.

obj.description:

- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: remove-from-context
tools.description:
Removes the object from the chat context.
Use this it for objects that are no longer useful for the conversation.

- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: load-object
tools.description:
Loads the object or relation content.
Can load multiple objects at at time.
Use the to read the data when you have a DXN.
Call this tool with an array of one or more DXNs or object IDs.
When use see a reference ({ '/': 'dxn:...' }), you can call this function to load the object.
Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

items.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: create-object
tools.description:
Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "dxn:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "dxn:queue:data:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: delete-object
tools.description: Deletes the object.

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: update-object

obj.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

- Organization "Tagged Corp": dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV4J

tools.name: query

Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).

  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.

  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.

    "text": "cyberdyne bob",

</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",

  }

I'll search for both the "obsolete" tag and the "Untagged Corp" organization simultaneously.

- Organization "Untagged Corp": `dxn:echo:BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U:01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV87`


Now I need to remove the "obsolete" tag from "Untagged Corp" since it's currently in that organization's metadata.
I found both the "obsolete" tag and the "Untagged Corp" organization. I can confirm the tag is currently applied. Let me remove it now.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

Note that returned data is only a snapshot in time, and might have changed since the object was last loaded.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

id.description: A Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier

jsonSchema.description: An organization.

extended.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region.

region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country.

postOfficeBoxNumber.description: The post office box number for PO Box addresses.

country.description: The country in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "dxn:echo:@:YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "dxn:echo:@:XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.
